title: Auth dependency family packet across seeded and live paths
description: Add a new outage family for auth dependency slowdown and token validation failures. Seeded incidents, live fresh incidents, and the operator story should all frame this family consistently without overclaiming runtime support before the bounded pack exists.
implementation_tasks: define the auth dependency outage family across seeded and live paths
add likely owner, customer path, and failure-story framing for auth dependency incidents
keep downgrade and evidence-posture language honest before runtime support is attached
make the new family buyer-legible in core product surfaces
done_when: the auth dependency family is coherent in seeded and live incident paths
operators can understand what kind of auth dependency problem is being framed
the product does not overclaim runtime support before the pack lands

title: Bounded REPLICA and TRACE support for auth dependency family
description: Add bounded reproduction and debugging support for the auth dependency family. The pack should stay narrow and curated, with explicit runtime-backed versus inferred-only behavior and a clear debugger packet that points engineering to the likely dependency boundary.
implementation_tasks: add a bounded runtime pack for the auth dependency family
attach REPLICA runtime posture and mitigation comparison for the new family
attach TRACE debugging packet and likely inspection path for the new family
preserve strict runtime-backed versus inferred-only language
done_when: the auth dependency family has bounded REPLICA and TRACE support where claimed
runtime comparison is visible and truthful for the new family
engineering gets a concrete but bounded debug handoff

title: Queue and worker backlog family packet across seeded and live paths
description: Add a fifth outage family for queue or worker backlog affecting transaction completion. The packet should explain customer impact, likely ownership, and the relay-reduction story across both seeded and live paths before deeper runtime support is attached.
implementation_tasks: define the queue or worker backlog family across seeded and live paths
add likely owner, customer path, and operational framing for queue backlog incidents
keep evidence posture honest when support is inference-first
make the family understandable in buyer and operator surfaces
done_when: the queue or worker backlog family is coherent in seeded and live paths
operators can understand the queue backlog problem frame
the product still reads as one support-triage category rather than a grab-bag

title: Bounded REPLICA and TRACE support for queue and worker backlog family
description: Add bounded reproduction and debugging support for the queue and worker backlog family. The runtime pack should stay narrow, with explicit mitigation ladders, runtime outcomes, and a developer handoff packet that points to the backlog-processing boundary.
implementation_tasks: add a bounded runtime pack for the queue or worker backlog family
attach REPLICA runtime posture and mitigation comparison for the family
attach TRACE debugging packet and inspection path for worker backlog issues
keep runtime-backed versus inference-only semantics strict
done_when: the queue or worker backlog family has bounded REPLICA and TRACE support where claimed
runtime comparison is visible and truthful for the new family
engineering gets a bounded but useful worker-backlog debug packet

title: Coverage matrix and pilot scorecard extension for the five-family wedge
description: Extend the tenant coverage matrix, scorecard, and proof surfaces so the five-family wedge is visible and bounded. Buyers and operators should be able to see exactly what is runtime-backed, inference-first, or unsupported across the expanded wedge.
implementation_tasks: extend coverage matrix for five-family support state
extend pilot scorecard and proof surfaces for the expanded wedge
keep runtime-backed versus inferred-only ratios honest as support broadens
make expanded wedge coverage understandable to buyers and operators
done_when: coverage and proof surfaces reflect the five-family wedge truthfully
operators can see what is covered per tenant without ambiguity
buyers can understand the widened wedge without the product losing focus

title: Fresh-incident routing and owner inference calibration across the five-family wedge
description: Calibrate fresh-incident framing, owner routing, and uncertainty posture now that the wedge covers five bounded families. The goal is better live routing quality without slipping into overconfident generic triage.
implementation_tasks: calibrate live routing and owner inference across five families
improve uncertainty language where multiple families overlap
keep fresh-incident evaluation coherent after wedge expansion
preserve downgrade honesty for weak or unsupported incidents
done_when: fresh incidents route more coherently across the five-family wedge
uncertainty language stays explicit when family overlap exists
the product remains honest on ambiguous or weak live incidents

title: Buyer and demo material refresh for the five supported outage families
description: Refresh the buyer-facing, owner-facing, and demo materials so the product can present the five-family wedge clearly. The demo story should stay narrow and legible while showing broader coverage and stronger runtime-backed reasoning.
implementation_tasks: refresh buyer proof and presentation material for five-family support
refresh demo walkthrough and owner-facing narrative for the widened wedge
keep the message tightly focused on support-to-engineering investigation workflow
make the expanded wedge easy to explain without sounding like a universal platform
done_when: buyer and demo material reflects five supported outage families
the market-facing narrative stays narrow and credible
owner-facing walkthroughs match the shipped product reality

title: Five-family wedge checkpoint and control-doc refresh
description: Close the selective expansion phase with a deliberate checkpoint. Validate the five-family wedge end-to-end, confirm seeded and live truthfulness, and refresh the control docs so the repo ends the phase with a clear bounded-wedge posture before ops maturity work begins.
implementation_tasks: run the full validation stack across tests, browser, demo, and Docker path
verify all five families remain bounded and truthful in seeded and live paths
refresh owner-facing control docs so the next active frontier is the ops-maturity phase
close the backlog cleanly before any 117–124 work begins
done_when: the five-family wedge is validated end-to-end
control docs truthfully describe the new bounded wedge baseline
the repo is ready to move into near-production ops maturity
